programs.name: Medical Assistance (Medicaid)
programs.what_it_does: Medical Assistance provides health coverage for low-income seniors, including doctor visits, hospital stays, and long-term care services. In Minnesota, it integrates with programs like Minnesota Senior Health Options (MSHO) and Minnesota Senior Care Plus (MSC+) for coordinated care.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65+ who meet income and asset limits; dual-eligible for Medicare and Medicaid in MSHO/MSC+.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; includes full Medicaid benefits plus Medicare integration in MSHO, no monthly premium[3][4].

programs.name: Elderly Waiver (EW)
programs.what_it_does: The Elderly Waiver funds home and community-based services (HCBS) to help seniors avoid nursing home placement. It provides services like home health aide, personal care assistance, and extended home care within budget caps.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ requiring nursing home level of care but preferring to stay at home; must be eligible for Medical Assistance.
programs.benefit_value: Services up to case mix budget caps and state rate limits (e.g., extended home care at specified units/durations)[2].

programs.name: Minnesota Senior Health Options (MSHO)
programs.what_it_does: MSHO combines Medicare and Medical Assistance into one plan with a care coordinator for health and support services. It covers medical care, drugs, preventive services, and HCBS for eligible seniors.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ eligible for both Medicare Parts A/B and Medical Assistance.
programs.benefit_value: Comprehensive Medicare + Medicaid benefits, no monthly premium; includes care coordination[3][4].

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs help pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for low-income seniors. Administered via Medical Assistance in MN, they include Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), and Qualifying Individual (QI).
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries meeting specific income/asset thresholds.
programs.benefit_value: Covers Part A/B premiums (up to $185/month for QI in 2026), deductibles, coinsurance; varies by program[6].

programs.name: Minnesota SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card to buy food for low-income households. Seniors may qualify for higher benefits due to deductions.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ or disabled with limited income/assets.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per person for seniors (2026 rates adjusted annually).

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP provides grants to help pay heating costs and address energy crises. It offers one-time payments to utilities or vendors.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors meeting income guidelines (up to 50% state median).

programs.name: Residential Energy Assistance Partnership (REAP) / Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: REAP provides emergency furnace repairs; Weatherization improves home energy efficiency with insulation and sealing. Both reduce energy costs for low-income homes.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors/homeowners or renters with energy burden.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $5,000 in furnace repairs (REAP); free weatherization services.

programs.name: Senior Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: SHIP offers free, unbiased counseling on Medicare, supplements, and drug plans. Counselors help with applications and appeals.

programs.name: Senior Nutrition Program (Meals on Wheels)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Also offers congregate meals at senior centers.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals; suggested contribution.

programs.name: Family Adult Assistance Program (FAA) / Alternative Care Grants
programs.what_it_does: Provides respite and caregiver support services through waivers like EW. Helps family caregivers with breaks and training.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors 65+ on EW/AC programs.
programs.benefit_value: Respite up to 40-80 hours/month within waiver budgets[2].

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time paid work training at community sites for low-income seniors. Builds skills for unsubsidized employment.

programs.name: Legal Assistance for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal help for issues like housing, benefits, abuse via Senior Legal Helpline. Represents in court if needed.

programs.name: Office of Ombudsman for Long-Term Care
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes/assisted living, investigates complaints. Ensures quality care and rights protection.

programs.name: UCare Minnesota Senior Care Plus (MSC+)
programs.what_it_does: MSC+ is a Medicaid health plan offering robust medical, mental health, pharmacy, and dental benefits, including waiver services for home-based care. It provides extras like no-cost office visits, 24/7 nurse line, disease management, fall prevention kits, healthy food discounts, and mobile dental care.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Minnesota residents aged 65 or older eligible for Medical Assistance (Medicaid), with or without Medicare Parts A and B, living in the UCare service area.[2]
programs.whats_unique: As part of Minnesota's tailored senior Medicaid plans, MSC+ includes state-specific waiver services and perks like food discounts via Healthy Benefits+ card and mobile dental, beyond basic federal Medicaid.[2]
programs.benefit_value: No-cost visits, prescription coverage, food discounts, rewards for screenings, mobile dental, and waiver services for independence.[2]

programs.name: Minnesota Aging Pathways (formerly Senior LinkAge Line)
programs.what_it_does: This free statewide service connects seniors to personalized resources for Medicare counseling, housing, financial support, long-term care options, and aging in place plans. Staffed by social workers and nurses, it offers preadmission screening for nursing facilities and long-term care consultations required for assisted living.[6][7]
programs.who_its_for: Minnesota seniors, families, and professionals seeking help with aging services, Medicare, housing, or home-based support; available M-F 8am-4:30pm.[7]
programs.whats_unique: A Minnesota Board on Aging program providing mandatory state-specific long-term care counseling and person-centered planning not replicated federally; partners with area agencies for comprehensive resource coordination.[6][7]
programs.benefit_value: Free expert counseling, resource plans, and screenings at no cost to help access services, financing, and housing options.[7]
